title: [azumarill] Battle Guide
summary: A [water]/[fairy] physical breaker whose [huge-power] doubles Attack outright, with [aqua-jet] priority and an optional [belly-drum] win condition.
strengths: [huge-power] doubles the base Attack to 200-plus effective damage
[water]/[fairy] STAB covers Dragon, Fighting, and Dark cleanly
[aqua-jet] provides priority to make up for low Speed
weaknesses: 50 Speed moves last against virtually everything
Electric, Grass, and Poison weaknesses are common in the format
Pre-setup damage is much weaker than post-setup damage

recommendedMoves.reason: [fairy] STAB for Dragon, Dark, and Fighting targets
+1 priority for cleaning up weakened attackers
Extreme setup move that maxes Attack in one turn

recommendedItems.reason: Triggers after to restore HP immediately
Boosts [water] STAB damage with no drawback

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[azumarill] is a [water]/[fairy] physical breaker whose entire case is built on the [huge-power] ability, which doubles Attack. Its base 50 Attack looks low on paper, but [huge-power] pushes it into effective 200+ territory, making [azumarill] one of the hardest-hitting physical attackers in the tier despite its unassuming stats. The role splits between a four-attack priority pivot and a [belly-drum] setup sweeper depending on team needs.

## Strengths
[huge-power] is the damage scaler that makes [azumarill] real. [liquidation] is the main STAB finisher with a defense-drop secondary, and [play-rough] handles Dragon, Dark, and Fighting attackers that would otherwise wall [water]. [aqua-jet] provides +1 priority, which fixes the Speed problem for cleaner duty. [belly-drum] is the win-condition move.

## Weaknesses & Answers
50 Speed is the limiting factor. Electric, Grass, and Poison are all weaknesses that common special attackers carry, which means bringing [azumarill] in on the wrong switch can end the slot before it attacks. [belly-drum] also cuts HP to 50 percent, so the setup window requires precise planning and [sitrus-berry] recovery.

## Recommended Moveset
[liquidation] is the locked [water] STAB with [huge-power] scaling. [play-rough] is the [fairy] STAB for Dragon and Fighting targets. [aqua-jet] is the priority slot that cleans up revenge-kill threats that would otherwise outspeed [azumarill]. [belly-drum] fills the setup slot in the dedicated sweeper build, or superpower replaces it for extra Rock and Steel coverage in the four-attack variant.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sitrus-berry] is the default for the [belly-drum] build — it triggers after the HP cost and restores a quarter of [azumarill]'s health, which is the difference between a committed sweeper and a wasted slot. [mystic-water] is the alternate for the four-attack build, boosting [water] STAB damage without locking moves. The ability must be [huge-power]. Tera [water] maxes [liquidation], and Tera [fairy] flips the defensive profile for [play-rough] focus.

## Teammates & Synergy
A slow pivot like a volt-switch user creates the safe entry turns [azumarill] needs to set up [belly-drum]. An Electric and Grass answer. Intimidate support on the back line keeps priority threats manageable, and a hazard remover preserves [azumarill]'s HP pool for [belly-drum]'s 50 percent cost.

## Play Patterns
For the [belly-drum] build, enter on a predicted swap, use [belly-drum] on the safe turn, trigger [sitrus-berry] recovery, and start chaining [liquidation] plus priority [aqua-jet] KOs. For the four-attack build, use [aqua-jet] as a revenge-kill tool and save [play-rough] for known Dragon or Fighting targets. Save the Tera specifically for the moment an Electric or Grass move is telegraphed.
